summary: Softwood lumber markets are experiencing their third tariff cycle since 2017. Canadian softwood faces a 14.54% countervailing duty currently under appeal, compressing margins for US importers. Pacific Northwest domestic mills are back at capacity after 2024 curtailments but output insufficient to cover demand surge. European spruce-fir (SPRUCE) is a viable alternative for framing grades, with 30–40 day Atlantic shipping. Brazilian plantation pine (Pinus elliottii) increasingly certified to FSC standards and competitive on commodity grades. Book forward contracts now - futures suggest 8–12% price increase through Q3.

suppliers.name: Weyerhaeuser Company
suppliers.supply_route_description: Vertically integrated - owns timberlands, mills, and distribution. Dimensional lumber, engineered wood (TJI joists, LVL). Reliable delivery from 35 owned mills.

suppliers.name: Canfor Corporation
suppliers.supply_route_description: Canada's largest softwood lumber producer. SPF (Spruce-Pine-Fir) and Douglas Fir from BC interior mills. Delivered via truck to US border crossing or rail. Note: 14.54% CVD applies.
suppliers.risk_notes: Subject to 14.54% US countervailing duty. Duty under WTO appeal; ruling expected late 2026. Duty currently passed through in pricing - factor into landed cost calculations.

suppliers.name: West Fraser Timber
suppliers.supply_route_description: Second-largest softwood producer in North America. Significant US South presence (acquired Norbord 2021). SPF from BC, SYP from US South - choose origin to optimize duty exposure.
suppliers.risk_notes: BC-origin product subject to 14.54% CVD. US South (SYP) product exempt from CVD - request SYP-origin specification to avoid tariff.

suppliers.name: Vida Group – Export Division
suppliers.supply_route_description: Swedish spruce and pine from FSC-certified forests. Kiln-dried and planed to US standard dimensions. Shipped via Gothenburg to US East Coast ports. 35–45 day lead time.

suppliers.name: Arauco – North America Export
suppliers.supply_route_description: Plantation pine (Pinus radiata from Chile, Pinus elliottii from Brazil). Radiata pine strong for framing; increasingly used in US engineered wood products. 45–60 day lead time to US Gulf/East Coast.

news_items.title: US Lumber Tariff on Canadian Softwood to Rise to 17.9% After Commerce Dept Review - Effective May 15
CME Lumber Futures Hit 18-Month High on Housing Start Surge and Tariff Uncertainty
